author: Chris Schommer
title: Christian Walker goes deep in win over Royals
desc: Christian Walker went 1-for-5 in a win over the Royals with a solo home run.
impact: Walker has heated back up at the plate, registering hits in eight of his last 10 contests. The first baseman continues to offer manager Joe Espada another explosive options in the Astros lineup, carrying an .817 OPS with 17 home runs, 40 runs scored, and 49 RBI across 70 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Brice Matthews homers in tight win over Royals
desc: Brice Matthews went 1-for-1 in a win over the Royals with a pinch-hit solo home run.
impact: Matthews has offered mixed results at the plate of late, picking up hits in four of his last nine contests. The 24-year-old remains one of the more inconsistent options when inserted in the Astros lineup for manager Joe Espada, slashing .209 with five homers, 16 RBI, 17 runs scored, and two stolen bases through 58 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Josh Hader locks down save as Astros outslug Royals
desc: Josh Hader tossed a scoreless inning to earn the save in Friday's 10-8 win over the Royals.
impact: Hader continues a strong stretch since returning from injury, holding opponents scoreless in each of his five trips to the mound. The left-hander remains an elite closer option for manager Joe Espada as part of an underwhelming Astros staff, posting a 0.00 ERA and 0.20 WHIP with two saves and a 12.6 K/9 ratio across five appearances.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Tatsuya Imai implodes early in win over Royals
desc: Tatsuya Imai allowed five earned runs on four hits and one walk while striking out one over 2/3 of an inning in Friday's win over the Royals.
impact: Imai has provided mixed results on the mound of late, allowing three earned runs or more in three of his last six contests. The right-hander remains a high-risk starting option in the Astros rotation due to underwhelming command, posting a 6.43 ERA and 1.51 WHIP with a 3-3 record and 1.5 K/BB ratio through nine starts.

author: Nate Miller
title: JR Ritchie excellent in long relief Friday night
desc: JR Ritchie worked five scoreless innings out of the bullpen in Friday's 7-5 loss to the Mets, allowing just two hits and one walk while striking out five.
impact: Ritchie entered this game in relief of Atlanta starter Spencer Strider during the fourth inning Friday night with one and none out. He was on the mound when Strider's last charged run came around to score, but other than that, Ritchie was on cruise control for the rest of the game. This was Ritchie's first appearance since being recalled earlier this week, and he certainly took advantage. He now holds a respectable 3.82 ERA with a 26-17 K/BB across 30 2/3 innings in the bigs this season. With Strider experiencing some arm discomfort Friday night, there's at least a chance Ritchie's next appearances comes as a starter.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Chase Meidroth racks up three hits, drives in two in win over Dodgers
desc: Chase Meidroth went 3-for-3 in a win over the Dodgers with a walk, one run scored, and two RBI.
impact: Meidroth continues an impactful stretch, getting on base in each of his last 22 contests. The infielder continues to provide manager Will Venable with one of the more steady options as part of a streaky White Sox lineup, batting .282 with five home runs, 23 RBI, 41 runs scored, and two stolen bases across 66 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Miguel Vargas picks up three hits in win over Dodgers
desc: Miguel Vargas went 3-for-4 in a win over the Dodgers with a double, one walk, one run scored, and one RBI.
impact: Vargas has caught fire at the plate, collecting hits in each of his last nine games. The 26-year-old remains one of the more stable options in a streaky White Sox lineup for manager Will Venable, posting an .878 OPS with 16 home runs, 44 RBI, 50 runs scored, and nine stolen bases across 67 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Andrew Benintendi goes yard as White Sox cruise past Dodgers
desc: Andrew Benintendi went 1-for-4 in a win over the Dodgers with a solo home run and one walk.
impact: Benintendi has offered mixed results at the plate of late, picking up hits in five of his last nine games. The 31-year-old remains one of many streaky options in the White Sox lineup for manager Will Venable, slashing .235 with seven homers, 31 RBI, 27 runs scored, and one stolen base through 61 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Sam Antonacci swipes base in win over Dodgers
desc: Sam Antonacci went 1-for-4 in Friday's 8-2 win over the Dodgers with a walk, a run scored, an RBI, and a stolen base.
impact: Antonacci has heated up of late, reaching base in six of his last eight contests. The outfielder has offered manager Will Venable an impactful option as part of a streaky White Sox lineup, batting .284 with one home run, 16 RBI, 31 runs scored, and nine stolen bases through 50 games.

author: Nate Miller
title: Matt Olson mashes 20th home run Friday night
desc: Matt Olson went 1-for-5 with a solo homer in Friday's 7-5 loss to the Mets.
impact: Make it an even 20 homers on the year for Olson, who is now one of just five players to reach that marks so far this season. The three-time All-Star is now batting .270 alongside a .905 OPS with 51 RBI through 69 games. He's among the top run producers in the league right now.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Anthony Kay grinds through five in win over Dodgers
desc: Anthony Kay allowed two earned runs on four hits and one walk while striking out seven over five innings in Friday's win over the Dodgers.
impact: Kay continues a strong stretch on the mound, allowing two earned runs or less in seven of his last eight starts. The left-hander continues to put a shaky start to the season in the rearview mirror, posting a 4.34 ERA and 1.42 WHIP to go with a 6-1 record and 7.2 K/9 ratio across 14 appearances.

author: Nate Miller
title: Andrew Painter labors behind opener Friday night
desc: Andrew Painter allowed five runs on five hits and three walks while striking out three over five innings in Friday's 6-0 loss to the Brewers.
impact: In an attempt to try to improve upon recent struggles, Painter shifted to pitching behind an opener Friday. It didn't really pay off. The rookie right-hander has now been tagged for 15 runs in just 13 innings over his last three outings, running his ERA for the year up to an ugly 6.43 to go with a 1.57 WHIP. The Phillies might soon decide to make a change at the back of their rotation.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Jacob Misiorowski dazzles in shutout win over Phillies
desc: Jacob Misiorowski allowed one hit while striking out 15 over nine scoreless innings in a complete game shutout win over the Phillies.
impact: Misiorowski continues a dominant stretch on the mound, allowing one earned run or less while striking out eight or more in each of his last eight trips to the mound. The right-hander continues to reward manager Pat Murphy with an elite starting option every fifth day as part of a strong Brewers top of the rotation, carrying a 1.34 ERA and 0.74 WHIP to go with an 8-2 record and 13.6 K/9 ratio across 14 starts.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Jake Bauers cashes in with three-run blast in win over Phillies
desc: Jake Bauers went 1-for-3 in a win over the Phillies with a three-run home run and one walk.
impact: Bauers continues a productive stretch at the plate, driving in runs in four of his last six contests. The 30-year-old offers manager Pat Murphy one of the more explosive bats in the Brewers lineup, carrying a .905 OPS with 13 home runs, 46 RBI, 40 runs scored, and five stolen bases across 59 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Brice Turang swipes base in win over Phillies
desc: Brice Turang went 1-for-4 in a win over the Phillies with a double, a run scored, and a stolen base.
impact: Turang continues a solid stretch at the plate, recording hits in nine of his last 11 contests. The second baseman continues to offer manager Pat Murphy one of the more consistent options in the Brewers lineup, batting .267 with 10 home runs, 42 RBI, 53 runs scored, and 12 stolen bases across 64 games.
